title: Stenogrammitis rupestris
taxonomy: Stenogrammitis rupestris is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Stenogrammitis, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with over 2,000 species. Stenogrammitis rupestris is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This species of fern is often found growing on trees and rocks in its native habitat.

intro: Stenogrammitis rupestris, also known as the rock fern or cliff fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Stenogrammitis rupestris has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stenogrammitis rupestris is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
